frameworks.name: subjective value coding in OFC
frameworks.one_line: OFC high-frequency activity tracks current and previous trial subjective values

frameworks.name: temporal context dependency
frameworks.one_line: value coding modulated by recent past offers, extended from monkey to human

frameworks.name: population trajectory / dimensionality reduction
frameworks.one_line: dynamic low-dimensional OFC trajectories reveal when past influences current

core_question: Do human OFC and related regions encode subjective value in a context-dependent manner influenced by recent past offers?
key_assumptions: high-frequency (gamma / high-gamma) power reflects local population activity
monkey context-dependent OFC findings can generalize to humans
manipulations: current-trial vs previous-trial subjective value as regressors on neural signals
snack food items with varying willingness-to-pay across trials
recording across OFC, insula, cingulate, parietal, amygdala, hippocampus, striatum
key_findings: OFC high-frequency power positively correlates with current subjective value
OFC high-frequency power negatively correlates with previous trial subjective value
most significant contacts encoded either present or past value, not both
population trajectories show past-trial influence emerging early after current offer onset
interpretations: human OFC exhibits temporal context dependency in value coding analogous to monkey OFC
past and present value are represented largely by separate sub-populations within OFC
